evals.prompt: Can you take a look at the subwave event logs and give me a rundown of what the station's been doing the last while? Want to know if anything's off. The state dir is at sample-state.
evals.expected_output: A written diagnostic report covering Navidrome usage, picker/DJ quality, and health & anomalies, grounded in the analyze_events.py output, with recommendations.
expectations: Ran scripts/analyze_events.py rather than parsing the JSONL file by hand
The report covers all three lenses: Navidrome usage, picker/DJ quality, and health & anomalies
The report identifies the failed station-id trace caused by an Ollama connection error (ECONNREFUSED)
The report flags the narrow library pool and artist repetition (Khruangbin / Men I Trust dominating airplay)
Findings are paired with concrete recommendations that include reasoning, not just observations
Figures cited in the report match the script output and are not invented

evals.prompt: my radio keeps playing khruangbin like every other song lol. can you dig into the logs and figure out why it's so repetitive? logs are under sample-state
evals.expected_output: A report that traces the repetition to a narrow candidate pool (not the agent disobeying), quotes the repetition figures, and recommends a concrete fix.
expectations: Ran scripts/analyze_events.py to analyse the log
Explains the repetition is caused by a narrow candidate pool / thin library coverage, not the picker ignoring its instructions
Quotes the artist-repetition statistic and/or the most-aired-artists breakdown from the digest
Gives a concrete recommendation such as checking library size, running the library tagger, or broadening the picker pool sources

evals.prompt: something feels slow with the radio and i keep seeing navidrome stuff scroll past in the logs. can you analyse how the navidrome api calls are being made and whether they look healthy? the sample-state folder has the logs
evals.expected_output: A report focused on Navidrome call patterns: per-endpoint volume and latency, the getSimilarSongs2 errors, and a recommendation addressing them.
expectations: Ran scripts/analyze_events.py to analyse the log
Identifies that getSimilarSongs2 is the slow endpoint and has a cluster of errors
Explains the 522 errors most likely mean NAVIDROME_URL points at the Cloudflare-fronted origin instead of the internal address
Reports per-endpoint call volume/latency or distinguishes traced calls from boot-time warm-up calls
Includes a recommendation that addresses the Navidrome errors
